top_stories.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
top_stories.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access to targeted systems.

top_stories.title: The death of standing privilege in the age of AI agents
top_stories.summary: The article examines how AI agents are acquiring elevated privileges that traditional Privileged Access Management (PAM) controls are not designed to handle. It argues that standing privilege models are inadequate for AI agent identities, pointing to the need for just-in-time access and dynamic authorization approaches.

top_stories.title: UNC6783 Hackers Use Fake Okta Pages in Corporate Breach Campaign
top_stories.summary: A threat actor tracked as UNC6783 is conducting a corporate breach campaign using fake Okta login pages combined with social engineering and impersonation of support staff to gain unauthorized access to corporate systems. The campaign involves credential harvesting through fraudulent Okta authentication pages to steal sensitive data from targeted organizations.

top_stories.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
top_stories.summary: Microsoft released a massive patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with privilege elevation bugs accounting for more than half of the total, including two zero-days. Elevation-of-privilege vulnerabilities are of direct concern to IAM and PAM professionals as they represent risks to access control and privileged account security.

top_stories.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
top_stories.summary: The article addresses the growing threat of credential-based attacks and how they evade traditional detection by mimicking normal business activity. It outlines detection model shifts cybersecurity teams need to adopt to identify and respond to identity-driven breaches.

news.title: Russia's 'Fancy Bear' APT Continues Its Global Onslaught
news.summary: Russia's Fancy Bear APT group continues widespread cyberattacks globally, with security experts recommending zero trust as a defensive measure. The article focuses primarily on the threat actor's espionage activities rather than specific IAM implementations or products.

news.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
news.summary: Specops outlines five ways Zero Trust architecture enhances identity security, focusing on limiting access and preventing privilege escalation from stolen credentials. The piece covers identity-first Zero Trust principles including device trust enforcement and lateral movement prevention.

news.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
news.summary: The FBI and Indonesian National Police dismantled the W3LL phishing network infrastructure and detained its alleged developer, who built a toolkit used to steal account credentials and attempt over $20 million in fraud. The W3LL phishing toolkit was sold as an off-the-shelf product specifically designed to bypass MFA and compromise Microsoft 365 accounts at scale.

news.title: Google Rolls Out DBSC in Chrome 146 to Block Session Theft on Windows
news.summary: Google has made Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) generally available to Windows users in Chrome 146, with macOS support planned for a future release. DBSC cryptographically binds browser sessions to a specific device, preventing session token theft and cookie hijacking attacks.

news.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "Starkiller" proxies real login pages in real time, relaying victims' credentials and MFA codes to legitimate sites to bypass multi-factor authentication protections. The service evades traditional anti-phishing takedowns by loading actual brand websites rather than static copies, making it a significant threat to MFA-based authentication defenses.

news.title: Show HN: Prmana – OIDC SSH Login for Linux with DPoP (Rust, Apache 2.0)
news.summary: Prmana is a new open-source tool written in Rust (Apache 2.0) that replaces static SSH keys with short-lived OIDC tokens validated at the host via PAM. The implementation uses DPoP (RFC 9449) to cryptographically bind tokens to the holder's private key, differentiating it from other OIDC-for-SSH approaches.

news.title: Bringing governance and visibility to machine and AI identities
news.summary: AppViewX CEO Archit Lohokare discusses the convergence of machine and AI agent identity management as a unified governance challenge. The interview draws on his experience at IBM and CyberArk to address visibility and governance approaches for non-human identities.

news.title: Google Chrome Update Disrupts Infostealer Cookie Theft
news.summary: Google has added Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) to Chrome 146, using hardware-based cryptographic keys to bind session cookies to specific devices on Windows. This security control directly counters infostealer malware that steals session cookies to bypass authentication, addressing a common technique used to circumvent MFA and hijack authenticated sessions.

news.title: To counter cookie theft, Chrome ships device-bound session credentials
news.summary: Google is shipping device-bound session credentials in Chrome to counter cookie theft attacks, where stolen authentication cookies are used to hijack authenticated sessions. The feature cryptographically binds session credentials to a specific device, preventing exfiltrated cookies from being usable on attacker-controlled systems.

news.title: Google Chrome 146 Adds Device Bound Session Credentials to Stop Session Cookie Theft on Windows
news.summary: Google has introduced Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) in Chrome 146 for Windows, a security feature designed to prevent session cookie theft. DBSC cryptographically binds browser sessions to a specific device, making stolen session cookies unusable on other machines.

news.title: Google Chrome adds infostealer protection against session cookie theft
news.summary: Google has rolled out Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) in Chrome 146 for Windows, a security feature that cryptographically binds session cookies to a specific device to prevent theft by infostealer malware. The protection works by tying authentication sessions to the local device using cryptographic keys, making stolen session cookies unusable on other machines.

news.title: 'Several dozen' high-value corporations hit by new extortion crew in helpdesk phishing spree
news.summary: A new extortion crew has compromised several dozen high-value corporations using phishing and helpdesk social-engineering attacks, according to Google. The attacks exploit helpdesk personnel as an attack vector to gain unauthorized access, directly targeting identity verification and access management processes.

news.title: Investigating Storm-2755: “Payroll pirate” attacks targeting Canadian employees
news.summary: Microsoft's DART researchers identified a financially motivated threat actor, Storm-2755, compromising Canadian employee accounts to gain unauthorized access to employee profiles and divert payroll funds. The attacks involve account compromise and unauthorized access to employee identity and HR systems, making it directly relevant to identity and access management security.
